nodes.name: Bicolored scrubwren
nodes.summary: The bicolored scrubwren or bicolored mouse-warbler (Aethomyias nigrorufus) is a species of bird in the family Acanthizidae. It is found in the New Guinea Highlands; its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests. This species was formerly placed in the genus Crateroscelis but following the publication of a molecular phylogenetic study of the scrubwrens and mouse-warblers in 2018, it was moved to the resurrected genus Aethomyias. References External links Image at ADW Archived 2008-07-24 at the Wayback Machine
